commit.message:
feat: replace 1-hour JWT auth tokens with permanent HMAC autologin codes

Newsletter links now use HMAC-based autologin codes (?ac=<hex>) instead of
Firebase custom tokens (?at=<jwt>). Key improvements:

- Never expires: HMAC code is deterministic, client exchanges it for a fresh
  Firebase token on-demand via Cloud Function API call
- Much shorter URLs: 67 chars (HMAC hex) vs 793 chars (JWT) — well under
  Mailgun's 1000-char click tracking limit
- No Firebase Admin SDK needed at send time: pure HMAC, no async token
  generation per subscriber

Changes:
- scripts/send-newsletter.mjs: Replace getOrCreateCustomToken() with
  generateAutologinCode() using HMAC('autologin:'+email, secret)
- functions/src/newsletterSubscriptionManagement.js: Add exchange_auth_code
  action that validates HMAC and returns fresh createCustomToken()
- functions/index.js: Support JSON responses from exchange_auth_code
- functions/src/newsletterConfirmationEmail.js: Remove JWT from confirmation
  URLs (Cloud Function already returns token in API response)
- App.tsx: Auto-login useEffect exchanges ac code via API; action handler
  supports ac for unsubscribe/resubscribe; skip auto-login for confirm action
- services/newsletterSubscribers.ts: Add exchangeNewsletterAuthCode()
- services/authService.ts: Check ac param in shouldWaitForAuth()
- build-plugins/constants.ts: Add ac to SPA redirect script

Backwards compatible: legacy at/authToken params still work for old emails.

commit.message:
watchdog: distinguish RPC busy from de-registration; add audit sections 14.11-14.12

- RPC timeout no longer immediately counts as de-registration.
  If timed has logged within DAEMON_ACTIVE_SECS (default 90s), the
  daemon is alive but busy (fork resolution, reconnection storm).
  Busy timeouts increment rpc_busy_streak; only after RPC_BUSY_MAX
  (default 10 consecutive polls, ~90s) without log activity does it
  escalate to a fail_streak increment and eventual restart.
- When busy: logs '⏳ RPC timeout — daemon is alive and logging
  (busy_streak: N/10); NOT counting as de-registration'
- When silent AND unresponsive: logs 'RPC call failed — daemon has
  NOT logged in 90s' and escalates normally.
- RPC_TIMEOUT default: 3s → 8s (more headroom during load spikes)
- FAIL_THRESHOLD default: 2 → 3 (confirmed not-active before restart)
- daemon_recently_active() function added (journalctl short-unix parse)
- rpc_busy_streak state variable + reset on RPC success
- Restart log message: 'detected' → 'confirmed' to reduce false alarm tone
- New --rpc-busy-max N and --daemon-active-secs N CLI flags
- docs: add sections 14.11 (tokio starvation via reconnection storm)
  and 14.12 (watchdog false-restart on RPC timeout) to security audit;
  add three new rows to summary attack surface table

Fixes the pattern seen Apr 8 2026: restarts #12-17 at 10-min intervals
triggered by RPC starvation from the fork-resolution busy-loop.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add process_shigella_data function for data reshaping

This function filters and reshapes longitudinal Shigella data for serodynamics workflows, standardizing column names and preparing the dataset for conversion.

* Add function to summarize antibody trajectories for newperson

This function summarizes population-level antibody trajectories for a hypothetical new individual, computing median and credible intervals. It allows customization of various parameters for plotting and data return options.

* Add function to predict posterior at specified times

This function generates posterior predictions of antibody trajectories at specified time points for selected subjects and antigen/isotype. It processes posterior draws and evaluates the antibody curve using specified parameters.

* Add compute_residual_metrics function for antibody data

This function computes residuals between observed antibody measurements and posterior median predictions, returning pointwise residuals or aggregated error metrics (MAE, RMSE, SSE) at various summary levels.

* Add model comparison function with residual metrics

This function computes and compares residual metrics for serotype-specific and overall models, providing insights on their performance differences.

* Add fmt_mci function to format median and intervals

* Add function to extract and summarize newperson parameters

This function processes posterior draws for the 'newperson' subject, filtering and reshaping the data into a wide format suitable for analysis.

fix(smoketest): prevent SIGPIPE from trivy killing script via EXIT trap

trivy --version outputs many lines; head -1 closes the pipe early causing
trivy to get SIGPIPE (exit 141). With set -eo pipefail this non-zero exit
fires the EXIT trap inside the $() subshell which calls cleanup/exit 0,
terminating the whole script. Add '|| true' to suppress the SIGPIPE.

commit.message:
Create shared SDK types (#298)

* Add wasm-meta-registry-types and rich API

Extract shared wire types into a new crate `wasm-meta-registry-types` and update the workspace/Cargo manifests. The client crate now depends on and re-exports the types crate; several new rich client methods were added (fetch_package_detail, fetch_package_versions, fetch_package_version, search by import/export) along with helper fetch_* functions. Server routes and handlers were added/nested to support package detail, versions, specific-version (reordered path), and interface-based search; routing was reorganized to avoid wildcard conflicts. The package manager and storage layers were updated to use the new types (including a ManifestRow helper) and new manager methods to return detailed package/version information. README and tests updated to reflect the move.

* Add package kind (component/interface) support

Introduce PackageKind enum and an optional kind field for KnownPackage and PackageDetail to track whether a package is a runnable component or a WIT interface. Persist kind in the database (added kind column and migration), update OCI upsert to accept/preserve kind (COALESCE), and wire the value through storage, manager, indexer and client code. Added parse_kind helper to map DB values to PackageKind and updated tests/examples to include kind = None for backward compatibility.

* more

* Fix Tracey coverage annotations for rich registry APIs

Allow CORS requests from www.metafaux.com (#42)

The frontend at `www.metafaux.com/klaxon` is blocked from loading API
data because `https://www.metafaux.com` is not in the CORS allowed
origins list.

## Changes

- **`.env.example`** — Added
`CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://www.metafaux.com`; production deployments
need this env var set to enable cross-origin access
- **`phpunit.xml`** — Injected `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` for the test
environment
- **`tests/Feature/CorsTest.php`** — Added CORS tests covering:
- Preflight (`OPTIONS`) and actual `GET` requests from the allowed
origin receive the correct `Access-Control-Allow-Origin` header
- Disallowed origins are not echoed back (browser will block the
request)

## How it works

`config/cors.php` already reads from `CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS` via `env()`:

```php
'allowed_origins' => array_values(array_filter(array_map('trim', explode(',', env('CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS', ''))))),
```

No config changes needed — only the environment variable was missing.

commit.message:
fix: Away Agent reactive task never replied due to broken config join + RPC

- reactive.ts: remove broken agent_configs embed join (no direct FK between
  agent_conversation_log and agent_configs). Query config separately per-log.
  This was causing config = null for every log, skipping all replies entirely.
- reactive.ts: add exchange_count pre-check to skip already-exhausted logs
  before calling OpenAI
- reactive.ts: widen reply window from 2 min to 5 min for task jitter tolerance
- agentEngine.ts: rewrite incrementExchangeCount — remove dead first update
  and non-existent increment_agent_exchange RPC. Use clean read-then-write
  with proper limit_reached status and ended_at timestamp.

commit.message:
feat(benefit-engine): Sprint 1 remediation — catalog, API, frontend & QA fixes

## Catalog Fixes
- Add variableAmounts JSONB field to MasterBenefit for variable monthly
  amounts (e.g., Amex Uber December $35 bonus)
- Fix Amex Gold Resy cadence from ANNUAL to SEMI_ANNUAL
- Add Prisma migration: 20260410000000_sprint1_variable_amounts

## API Fixes
- Add periodStatus filtering to 4 endpoints: dashboard/benefits,
  cards/[id], benefits/recommendations, mobile/sync
- Add hydratePeriodFields utility for manual-add and import flows
  to compute correct period boundaries without engine running
- Add server-side edit/delete guards on benefits/[id] route to
  prevent mutation of engine-managed benefits

## Frontend Fixes
- SummaryStats: deduplicate by composite key (masterBenefitId +
  cardId) to fix double-counting in spouse/multi-card scenarios
- Dashboard benefit card: deduplicate benefit list before rendering
- Card detail page: use unique count for benefit totals
- EditBenefitModal: render read-only mode for engine-managed benefits

## QA Fixes
- Fix Prisma relation name mismatch between schema and queries
- Fix dedup composite key to handle spouse scenario correctly
- Add DELETE guard to prevent deletion of engine-managed benefits

## Infrastructure
- Add BENEFIT_ENGINE_ENABLED=false to railway.json (safe default)
- Document feature flag in .env.example
- Add backfill and cadence-fix scripts for production data migration
- Add benefit-engine specs and QA reports

## Safety
- Feature flag BENEFIT_ENGINE_ENABLED remains OFF (false)
- Zero-risk deployment: all existing behavior unchanged until flag enabled
- Engine-managed benefit guards prevent accidental data corruption
